source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original single-body topology-optimization brief (Frustum Generate 2017 quadcopter challenge). No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a quadcopter frame per the topology-optimization study 'Generate Quadcopter' open-CAD challenge (2017), delivered as a multi-part bolted assembly rather than a single monolithic body. Frame targets: total frame mass <= 90 g; support 4 BLDC motors symmetrically arranged on a 250 mm motor-to-motor diagonal; hold a 1000 mAh 3S Li-Po battery at a central location (battery mass 80 g, dimensions 73 x 34 x 16 mm); four ESCs mounted adjacent to motors; full-vehicle mass (frame + electronics + battery + propellers) 450 g; thrust-to-weight ratio T/W >= 2:1 so total thrust >= 9 N (approx 2.25 N per motor). Material: carbon-fiber-reinforced PA12 (nylon 12 with 40% short carbon fiber), 3D printed (MJF or SLS), E = 5 GPa, yield = 110 MPa, ultimate = 130 MPa, density = 1200 kg/m^3. Geometric constraints: motor mount pads 16 mm circle with 19 mm bolt-hole pattern (4x M3); central battery pocket 75 x 36 x 17 mm; overall frame bounding 280 mm x 280 mm x 25 mm. Load cases: (R1) Normal flight 1 g hover (forces balance, low stress): not binding but verified. (R2) 2 g pull-up: full 450 g x 2 g x 9.81 = 8.8 N distributed to four motor mounts = 2.2 N per mount upward; max von Mises in frame arm root <= yield / 2.0 = 55 MPa. (R3) Hard landing 10 g: 450 g x 10 g x 9.81 = 44 N at battery mount pushing down; max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 73 MPa. (R4) Crash impact: 50 N at one motor boom tip horizontally (prop strike); max von Mises <= ultimate / 1.5 = 87 MPa. (R5) Frame mass <= 90 g. (R6) First modal frequency cantilevered at battery mount / central hub >= 80 Hz (to avoid coupling with motor 1P frequency at approximately 40 Hz at 2400 RPM). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x3 cases) + modal. Vibration secondary via R6. Thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid, build orientation, and a report listing frame mass in g, max von Mises per case in MPa, first modal frequency in Hz.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The frame is built from the following part families: (A) top center hub plate 80 x 80 x 2 mm; (B) bottom center hub plate 80 x 80 x 2 mm sandwiching the battery tray and arms; (C) four radial arms connecting hub to motor stations (length ~125 mm, tapered I-section); (D) four motor mount pads 24 mm OD with 19 mm PCD M3 pattern at arm tips; (E) two longitudinal landing skid supports beneath the bottom plate; (F) battery tray 75 x 36 x 17 mm clipped between the hub plates; (G) antenna mast bracket on the top plate; (H) forward-facing camera/gimbal bracket on the bottom plate; (I) M3 fastener set (4x per motor pad + 4x per hub-to-arm joint = 32 fasteners) securing motor pads, arm roots, and landing skids. Interfaces: arm roots bolted between top/bottom hub plates (IF1, IF2); motor pads bolted to arm tips (IF3); landing skids bolted to bottom plate (IF4); battery tray clipped into hub plates (IF5); antenna and gimbal brackets bolted to their respective hub plate (IF6, IF7). FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED multi-body model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC4 prop-strike at motor boom tip). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design a quadcopter frame per the topology-optimization study Generate Quadcopter open-CAD challenge (2017), delivered as a multi-part bolted assembly. Total frame mass <= 90 g; support 4 BLDC motors symmetrically arranged on a 250 mm motor-to-motor diagonal; hold a 1000 mAh 3S Li-Po battery at a central location (battery mass 80 g, dimensions 73 x 34 x 16 mm); four ESCs adjacent to motors; full-vehicle mass 450 g; thrust-to-weight T/W >= 2:1 so total thrust >= 9 N (~2.25 N per motor).

Material: carbon-fiber-reinforced PA12 (nylon 12 with 40% short carbon fiber), 3D printed (MJF or SLS), E = 5 GPa, yield = 110 MPa, ultimate = 130 MPa, density 1200 kg/m^3. Motor mount pads 16 mm circle with 19 mm bolt-hole pattern (4x M3); central battery pocket 75 x 36 x 17 mm; overall frame bounding 280 x 280 x 25 mm.

LC1 hover 1 g (not binding, verified). LC2 pull-up 2 g: 450 g x 2 g x 9.81 = 8.8 N distributed to 4 motor mounts (2.2 N each); max von Mises at arm root <= yield / 2.0 = 55 MPa. LC3 hard landing 10 g: 44 N at battery mount; max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 73 MPa. LC4 crash impact: 50 N horizontal at one motor boom tip; max von Mises <= ultimate / 1.5 = 87 MPa. Frame mass <= 90 g. First modal frequency cantilevered at battery mount / central hub >= 80 Hz (motor 1P ~40 Hz at 2400 RPM).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 90 g mass cap is aggressive; Frustum challenge explicitly scored on generative design.

prompt.title: Frustum Generative Quadcopter frame (multi-part assembly: hub plates + arms + motor mounts + landing skids + battery tray + brackets + fasteners)
prompt.brief: CF-PA12 multipart quadcopter frame <= 90 g, 250 mm diagonal, 450 g vehicle, T/W >= 2:1. Two stacked hub plates sandwich 4 radial arms; motor mount pads at each arm tip; 2 landing skid supports beneath; battery tray, antenna mast and camera gimbal brackets; M3 fastener set. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under LC4 governing load.

boundary_conditions.mounting: central battery mount constrained for modal; motor-mount pads fixed for structural
boundary_conditions.loads: per-motor or central battery; assembly loads flow arm-tip -> arm -> hub plates -> battery tray

parts.name: top_center_plate
parts.role: upper hub plate sandwiching the arm roots and carrying the antenna mast

parts.name: bottom_center_plate
parts.role: lower hub plate clamping the arm roots, battery tray, and camera/gimbal bracket

parts.name: arm
parts.role: radial arm from hub to motor station, carrying thrust and prop-strike tip load

parts.name: motor_mount_pad
parts.role: circular mounting pad at each arm tip for BLDC motor

parts.name: landing_skid_support
parts.role: longitudinal landing skid beneath bottom plate carrying the 10 g hard-landing reaction

parts.name: battery_tray
parts.role: battery pocket tray clipped between the hub plates carrying the 80 g Li-Po

parts.name: antenna_mast_bracket
parts.role: vertical antenna mast bracket on top plate

parts.name: camera_gimbal_bracket
parts.role: forward-facing camera/gimbal bracket on bottom plate

parts.name: fastener_set
parts.role: M3 fasteners securing motor pads, arm roots, and landing skids

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 280 x 280 x 25 mm (tip-to-tip 250 mm motor diagonal)
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +Z up; 4 arms at 45, 135, 225, 315 deg about hub center

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC4 prop-strike tip).

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.
Generated coverage gate: ensures the declared load case is present in the CCX deck and checked from model.dat.

interfaces.load_path: arm root bolted through top plate into bottom plate, sandwich clamp
arm root clamped by bottom plate + top plate sandwich
motor thrust and prop-strike load at arm tip into motor mount pad
hard-landing reaction from skids into bottom plate
battery inertial load into hub plates under LC3

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Arm-root joints and motor-pad bolts transfer the governing prop-strike load between families, so per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Printed-nylon quadcopter frames in this class are typically assembled from two stacked hub plates sandwiching radial arms, bolted motor-mount pads, separate landing skids, and a clipped battery tray rather than a single monolithic print.
notes.speculation_caveats: Arm count (4), motor-pad bolt pattern, and bracket placement follow the original brief; landing-skid count (2), antenna and camera bracket inclusion, and fastener count are programmatic speculation.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
